title: BTS WORLD TOUR 'ARIRANG' Opens in Goyang — Largest K-pop Concert Tour in History
detail: BTS officially opens the ARIRANG World Tour on April 9, 2026 at Goyang Stadium, South Korea — their first full headline concert in three and a half years. Despite rain, 40,000+ fans pack the venue for a landmark night described by members as 'a totally different stage than before.' The show features a 360-degree in-the-round stage with no designated front section, allowing all angles of the audience equal proximity. The opening night is the first of three Goyang dates (April 9, 11, 12), all sold out. A global live viewing simultaneously streams to theaters in 35+ countries via Fandango and local exhibitors. The Arirang World Tour spans 85+ shows across 34 cities in 23 countries through March 2027 — described by HYBE as the largest concert tour by a Korean act in history. Korea Times, Yonhap, and international outlets confirm the tour launched with a 34-song setlist including ARIRANG album tracks alongside classic BTS catalog hits.

media.caption: BTS launches ARIRANG World Tour in Goyang, April 9 2026

title: Arirang World Tour Live Viewing Screens Globally in 35+ Countries via Fandango
detail: Concurrent with the opening night at Goyang Stadium, BTS's ARIRANG World Tour is live-streamed to movie theaters in 35+ countries on April 9, 2026 via Fandango and partner distributors — the largest simultaneous live viewing event in K-pop history. A second theater live viewing from the Tokyo Dome leg is scheduled for April 18. According to Rolling Stone and Billboard, tickets are available at btsliveviewing.com and Fandango.com, with the offer running through April 21. The theater format features the full 360-degree stage experience, with a 2-hour runtime from the Goyang Stadium production.
